title: loeselia glandulosa var. nepetifolia
intro: Loeselia glandulosa var. nepetifolia, also known as False Catmint or Narrow-leaved Loeselia, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Its medicinal properties include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions, offering relief and promoting overall well-being. Loeselia glandulosa var. nepetifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal benefits, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies.
